title: Mustardy Smoky Russian Shrimp Pilaf
description: A flavorful and nutritious Russian-inspired dish made with shrimp paste, featuring a smoky and slightly spicy flavor.
instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the mustard seeds, coriander seeds, turmeric powder, and cumin powder, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the shrimp paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the shrimp and cook until pink and fully cooked, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the cooked rice, chopped cilantro, lemon juice, and salt to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple salad.
Reheat the pilaf in the microwave or on the stovetop as needed.
Use leftover pilaf as a filling for wraps or sandwiches.
Freeze the pilaf for up to 3 months for a quick and easy meal.
